package handler

import (
	"errors"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for domain_errors_declared_in_handler_package.
// Rule intent: Business error types declared only in handlers instead of a
// reusable domain or service boundary.

// These domain errors are declared in the handler package, which is the anti-pattern.
// They should live in a domain or errors package so services and repositories can use them.
var (
	ErrUserNotFound     = errors.New("user not found")
	ErrOrderNotFound    = errors.New("order not found")
	ErrInvalidEmail     = errors.New("invalid email address")
	ErrInsufficientFunds = errors.New("insufficient funds")
	ErrPaymentFailed    = errors.New("payment processing failed")
	ErrForbidden        = errors.New("forbidden")
	ErrUnauthorized     = errors.New("unauthorized")
)

type UserHandler struct{}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	err := ErrUserNotFound
	_ = err
}

func (h *UserHandler) ValidateEmail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	err := ErrInvalidEmail
	_ = err
}

type OrderHandler struct{}

func (h *OrderHandler) GetOrder(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	err := ErrOrderNotFound
	_ = err
}

func (h *OrderHandler) ProcessPayment(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	err := ErrInsufficientFunds
	_ = err
}
